system_prompt:
You are a senior dermoscopy expert and knowledge engineer.

You will be shown a candidate rule that fires correctly on some images (TRUE POSITIVES)
but incorrectly on others (FALSE POSITIVES). Your task is to identify the single most
discriminating visual feature that distinguishes the TP images from the FP images —
i.e., a feature that is consistently present in TPs but absent in FPs, or vice versa.

This feature will be used to tighten the rule's pre-conditions.

Output ONLY a JSON object:
{
  "discriminating_feature": "snake_case_feature_name",
  "description": "Plain-language description of the feature.",
  "present_in": "tp" | "fp",
  "confidence": "high" | "medium" | "low",
  "rationale": "Why this feature distinguishes TPs from FPs."
}

If you cannot identify a reliable discriminating feature, output:
{
  "discriminating_feature": null,
  "description": "Cannot identify a reliable discriminating feature.",
  "present_in": null,
  "confidence": "low",
  "rationale": "Explanation of why the distinction cannot be reliably made."
}
text_content:
Pair: Melanoma vs Melanocytic Nevus
Rule favors: Melanoma

Rule: When a melanocytic lesion shows diffuse pink-white or milky-white regression areas intermixed with fine dark granular peppering (fine punctate dark dots distributed across the lesion), and the overall pigmentation pattern is disorganized/granular rather than a well-formed organized reticular network, classify as Melanoma.

Pre-conditions:
  1. Lesion contains pink-white or milky-white areas within the body of the lesion (not just at the border) — regression structures
  2. Fine dark granular peppering visible: multiple tiny punctate dark dots scattered across the lesion, not organized into globules or a network
  3. Overall pigmentation is diffuse and granular/dusty in texture, lacking a well-organized honeycomb reticular network
  4. At least three color components present: light brown, medium/dark brown, AND pink-white/milky
  5. Lesion is asymmetric
  6. No seborrheic keratosis features (no milia-like cysts, no comedo-like openings, no sharp 'stuck-on' border)
  7. Lesion is flat or minimally elevated — not a raised dome-shaped or nodular lesion (regression-peppering pattern applies to superficial spreading morphology, not nodular melanoma)
  8. No features suggesting a non-melanocytic lesion: no red/purple lacunae (hemangioma), no central white patch with peripheral pigment ring (dermatofibroma)

TRUE POSITIVE cases (rule fired correctly):
  [TP 1] Ground truth: Melanoma
    Validator observations: The lesion shows: (1) diffuse pink-white/milky-white regression areas intermixed throughout the lesion body; (2) fine dark granular peppering — multiple tiny punctate dark dots scattered across the lesion without forming a clear network or globules; (3) disorganized, dusty/granular pigmentation lacking a well-formed reticular honeycomb network; (4) multiple color components including light brown, medium-dark brown, and pink-white; (5) asymmetric shape; (6) no milia-like cysts, comedo-like openings, or stuck-on borders to suggest seborrheic keratosis; (7) lesion appears flat/superficial (consistent with superficial spreading morphology); (8) no lacunae or dermatofibroma features. All pre-conditions are met.
  [TP 2] Ground truth: Melanoma
    Validator observations: The lesion shows: (1) a central pink-white/milky area within the lesion body consistent with regression; (2) fine dark granular peppering - multiple tiny punctate dark dots scattered across especially the peripheral brown areas; (3) diffuse granular/dusty pigmentation without a well-organized reticular network; (4) multiple colors including light brown, medium/dark brown, and pink-white; (5) clear asymmetry in shape and color distribution; (6) no milia cysts, comedo openings, or stuck-on appearance to suggest seborrheic keratosis; (7) lesion appears flat/superficial consistent with superficial spreading morphology; (8) no lacunae or dermatofibroma-like features. All pre-conditions are met.

FALSE POSITIVE cases (rule fired on wrong class):
  [FP 1] Ground truth: Melanocytic Nevus
    Validator observations: The lesion shows: (1) pink-white/milky regression areas intermixed within the lesion body, particularly at the periphery; (2) fine dark granular peppering — multiple tiny punctate dark dots scattered throughout; (3) disorganized, diffuse granular pigmentation without a well-formed honeycomb reticular network; (4) multiple color components including light brown, medium/dark brown (near-black centrally), and pink-white areas; (5) clear asymmetry in shape and color distribution; (6) no seborrheic keratosis features (no milia-like cysts, comedo openings, or sharp stuck-on border); (7) the lesion appears flat/superficial (consistent with superficial spreading morphology); (8) no lacunae or dermatofibroma features. All pre-conditions are met.

What single visual feature most reliably distinguishes the TP cases from the FP cases? Focus on what the validator *observed* in each case.
